title: bidens occidentalis
intro: Bidens occidentalis, also known as Western Marsh Elder or Western Beggar-ticks, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and distinctive seed heads. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Bidens occidentalis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Bidens occidentalis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
